stemHtml: The icing in cloud which forms over hills is likely to be more severe than in the same type of cloud over level terrain because:

options.a: enforced ascent of air releases more water, which is retained in the cloud by the increased upward components.
options.b: adiablatic cooling lowers the freezing level and the water content of the cloud.
options.c: increases the temperature inside the cloud by forcing the release of latent heat so causing the air to hold more water vapour.
options.d: orographic lifting causes the freezing level to rise and increases the free water content of the cloud.
